sumzero_ideas.notes: Local SumZero Ideas archive index; raw documents stay out of git.

terminalvalue_candidates.notes: Provider candidates for fundamentals, filings, transcripts, ownership, news, macro and valuation feeds.

research_archive.notes: Full raw insight records are kept out of the front dashboard payload.

selected_tools.name: Better EDGAR
selected_tools.dashboard_role: SEC filing retrieval and cleaner document discovery

terminalvalue.title: Better EDGAR
terminalvalue.description: The EDGAR you wish existed — cross-issuer search, a live filings feed, enriched 8-K/6-K exhibits, Form 4 + 13F, and email/RSS/JSON/MCP/API delivery.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Filings news feed + cross-issuer search (no ticker required)
terminalvalue.best_for: monitoring sec filing activity and market developments across multiple issuers or sectors simultaneously
terminalvalue.overview: sec filing dashboard and search tool that surfaces recent 8-k/6-k filings, insider form 4 trades, and 13f holdings across all issuers without requiring a ticker first. supports cross-issuer filtering by market cap, sector, form type, 8-k item, and exhibit label; delivers results via web, email digest, rss, json api, or mcp integration to chatgpt/claude.

selected_tools.name: Daloopa
selected_tools.dashboard_role: normalized KPIs and model-ready company data

terminalvalue.title: Daloopa
terminalvalue.description: AI-extracted, audit-ready financial data for 5,500+ global tickers — every number hyperlinked to its primary source for one-click verification.
terminalvalue.key_feature: >99% accurate, source-linked financials with Excel add-in
terminalvalue.best_for: analysts at hedge funds, mutual funds, and investment banks building and updating financial models — also supports builders creating financial ai agents.
terminalvalue.overview: ai-extracted fundamental data for 5,500+ global public companies, delivered via excel add-in, api, mcp server, and data sheets. every data point hyperlinked to its original source filing (sec, press releases, investor presentations, transcripts) for one-click verification. covers 14 years of history with 4-10x more kpis per ticker than other providers, updated during earnings with >99% accuracy.

terminalvalue.title: EDGAR Analyst
terminalvalue.description: Pulls SEC filings from EDGAR, extracts structured data, redlines disclosure changes across periods, and generates cited analysis within 60 seconds of filing publication
terminalvalue.key_feature: word-level redlines across filing periods with materiality scoring
terminalvalue.overview: pulls sec filings from edgar, extracts structured line items from tables/segments/footnotes, redlines word-level changes across filing periods with materiality scoring, and generates cited analysis within 60 seconds of publication. runs monitors on any ticker that trigger custom analysis on new filings and deliver results via email/slack/webhook before press releases land.
terminalvalue.best_for: hedge fund analysts, sell-side researchers, family office portfolio managers, and private equity teams reading sec filings under time pressure.
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Free reader tier visible, paid plans with 7-day trial mentioned but no numeric pricing shown on any page; pricing inferred as FREE-$$ based on freemium SaaS pattern; SOC2 Type II in progress signals p

selected_tools.name: Earnings API
selected_tools.dashboard_role: earnings calendar and reported earnings coverage

terminalvalue.title: Earnings API
terminalvalue.description: Provides REST API access to historical and upcoming earnings calendar data for NYSE and NASDAQ stocks with date-based queries, post-earnings reactions, and structured JSON responses for automated workflows.
terminalvalue.key_feature: date-based earnings calendar with beat/miss data plus post-report price/volume
terminalvalue.overview: rest api returning historical and upcoming earnings calendar data for nyse and nasdaq stocks. date-based queries for pre/after-market earnings schedules, estimate vs. actual eps and revenue, plus optional post-earnings price/volume reactions. structured json for backfills, daily syncs, and automation workflows.
terminalvalue.best_for: fintech products building earnings alerts, newsletters, quant dashboards, or research platforms that need calendar imports and scheduled event syncs.
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Free tier 100/day, 1k/mo. Paid tiers not shown but implied by 'Paid access' mention. Company (ToS, privacy, dashboard infra). Earnings calendar + reactions = core product.

selected_tools.name: FRED MCP
selected_tools.dashboard_role: macro series for rates, spreads, inflation, liquidity and commodities

terminalvalue.title: Financial Datasets
terminalvalue.description: Stock-market data API built for AI agents — financials, SEC filings, KPIs, insider trades, and analyst estimates across 17,000+ tickers and 30+ years of history.
terminalvalue.key_feature: API designed natively for LLM agents (not human dashboards)
terminalvalue.best_for: ai financial agents, quantitative research platforms, portfolio management systems, and event-driven trading infrastructure
terminalvalue.overview: stock-market data api serving real-time and historical financials, sec filings, kpis, insider trades, institutional holdings, and price data across 27,000+ us tickers with 30+ years of history. designed for programmatic consumption by ai agents and llm pipelines — structured json responses, section-level filing access, data available within seconds of publication.

selected_tools.name: Fiscal AI
selected_tools.dashboard_role: fundamental research and company model enrichment

terminalvalue.title: Fiscal AI
terminalvalue.description: Conversational AI research over institutional-grade financials for 100k+ global companies, powered by S&P Market Intelligence.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Natural-language queries across S&P-grade fundamentals + segment KPIs
terminalvalue.best_for: investors needing natural-language access to s&p-grade company financials and segment data
terminalvalue.overview: conversational ai interface for querying institutional-grade financial data on 100k+ global companies. uses natural language to search s&p market intelligence fundamentals and segment-level kpis.

selected_tools.name: Quartr
selected_tools.dashboard_role: earnings call transcripts, investor events and presentation coverage

terminalvalue.title: Quartr
terminalvalue.description: Live earnings calls, transcripts, slides, filings, and analyst estimates for 14k+ public companies — mobile, web, and API.
terminalvalue.key_feature: Live earnings audio + real-time transcripts for 14k+ companies
terminalvalue.best_for: finance professionals, financial institutions, research platforms, and ai companies needing structured investor relations data
terminalvalue.overview: ai infrastructure for company research providing live earnings calls, transcripts, filings, and slides from 14k+ public companies structured for ai. two offerings: quartr pro (ai research platform for finance professionals) and quartr api (ir data layer for builders), delivering real-time first-party data across 65+ markets.

selected_tools.name: ROIC AI
selected_tools.dashboard_role: quality, returns on capital and valuation context

terminalvalue.title: ROIC AI
terminalvalue.description: Financial analysis platform with 30+ years of statements, transcripts, screener, and a powerful API for 60k+ tickers.
terminalvalue.key_feature: 30-yr financials + ROIC-focused metrics with API & Sheets add-in
terminalvalue.best_for: investors who want comprehensive historical financials and ROIC-focused fundamental analysis in one place, and developers building applications with a financial data API.
terminalvalue.overview: financial data platform and API serving 30+ years of statements, pre-calculated fundamental ratios (ROIC, ROE, debt-to-EBITDA), earnings call transcripts, and stock prices for 60,000+ public companies across 70+ global exchanges. delivers data as JSON or TSV for spreadsheets; includes web interface for quick company lookup and AI assistant for queries.

selected_tools.name: Stock Titan
selected_tools.dashboard_role: company news and press release monitoring

terminalvalue.title: Stock Titan
terminalvalue.description: Provides real-time stock news feed with AI sentiment and impact scoring, momentum alerts, SEC filings monitoring, and keyword-based filtering for individual equities.
terminalvalue.key_feature: real-time AI sentiment & impact scoring (1-5 scale) on every news article
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing from homepage/pricing: Free, $19.99/mo Silver, $59.99/mo Gold, $89.99/mo Platinum. Highest visible = $89.99/mo = $$. Range: FREE-$$. Asset_class: focuses on individual stock tickers (NYSE/NASD
terminalvalue.best_for: traders who need to read company press releases and filings the moment they're filed, before price moves fully reflect the news
terminalvalue.overview: stock titan delivers a real-time news feed filtered to individual equities, with AI (rhea) scoring each article for sentiment and impact on a 1-5 scale. includes momentum scanner (argus) that flags stocks moving on breaking news, SEC filings monitoring with AI summaries, and keyword/float/market-cap/price filters to build custom feeds.

selected_tools.name: Unusual Whales
selected_tools.dashboard_role: options flow, unusual volume and insider/trading context

terminalvalue.title: Unusual Whales
terminalvalue.description: Tracks real-time options flow, dark pool activity, congressional trading, Greek exposure, and unusual stock market data via web platform and API with 100+ endpoints.
terminalvalue.key_feature: real-time options flow tracking with dark pool and congressional trading data
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing page meta exists but no visible numeric tiers extracted; multiple product tiers mentioned (free tier, API at $250/mo historical option trades, Discord bot) but exact monthly pricing not visibl
terminalvalue.best_for: retail traders spotting institutional moves and analyzing options flow
terminalvalue.overview: unusual whales tracks real-time options flow, dark pool activity, congressional trading, greek exposure, and unusual stock market data. delivers via web platform, mobile app, api with 100+ endpoints, websocket/kafka streaming, and mcp server integration. covers 8,000+ tickers with real-time and historical data.

terminalvalue.title: ValueInvesting.io
terminalvalue.description: Generates automated discounted cash flow valuations and intrinsic value estimates for over 45,000 stocks across 60 global exchanges using public data and consensus forecasts.
terminalvalue.key_feature: pre-calculated DCF models for all global stocks with modifiable assumptions
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: pricing from $14.99/mo Regional ($$) and $42.99/mo Global ($$$) annual plans; no enterprise tier pricing visible; company entity implied by team/contact structure
terminalvalue.best_for: value investors seeking pre-calculated intrinsic value models to screen undervalued stocks globally without building dcfs manually
terminalvalue.overview: automated dcf valuations for 45,000+ stocks across 60 global exchanges using public data, damodaran estimates, and consensus forecasts. includes pre-built models with modifiable assumptions, stock screener, ai-powered filings search, and unlimited data export. offers regional and global access tiers.

terminalvalue.title: WhaleWisdom
terminalvalue.description: Tracks institutional investor 13F filings, insider transactions, 13D/13G disclosures, and mutual fund holdings with backtesting and portfolio replication tools
terminalvalue.key_feature: 20+ years of 13F data with fund-level backtesting and consensus holdings analysis
terminalvalue.auto_add_notes: Pricing from /pricing: Free tier (2 years data) + Standard $90/quarter + Pro $150/quarter. Pro = $$. Enterprise = contact sales. API access on Standard+. Upper bound = $150/qtr = $50/mo = $$.
terminalvalue.best_for: analyzing and replicating institutional investor portfolios using quarterly 13F and Schedule 13D/G data.
terminalvalue.overview: tracks 13F filings, 13D/13G disclosures, insider transactions, and mutual fund holdings for institutional investors and hedge funds. offers 20+ years of historical data, backtesting tools to evaluate fund performance, and portfolio replication. provides web interface, excel add-in, screeners, and a developer API with digital signature authentication.

META.title: Beyond the Trillion-Dollar Club: 3 High-Growth Tech Stocks to Buy Right Now
META.claim: The article highlights three high-growth tech stocks outside the trillion-dollar club with significant upside potential: Sandisk (NAND memory for data centers), Nebius (AI data center infrastructure), and CoreWeave (AI computing resources). All three companies are experiencing rapid revenue growth driven by massive demand for AI and data center infrastructure, with projected growth rates significantly exceeding market averages.

META.title: Nebius Is Up 210% This Year. Here's Why There's Still Room for More Upside in 2026.
META.claim: Nebius, a Nvidia-backed neocloud AI computing platform company, has surged 210% year-to-date and is expected to continue growing significantly. With Q1 revenue up 684% year-over-year and Wall Street projecting 550% growth for 2026 and 225% for 2027, the company's potential may not be fully priced in yet. Major clients including Microsoft and Meta are driving this explosive growth.

META.title: Is Microsoft a Deep Value Stock?
META.claim: Microsoft stock has declined nearly 20% year-to-date while tech peers rally, but the company's strong fundamentals remain intact. With AI business revenue at a $37 billion annual run rate (123% YoY growth) and a P/E ratio of 23.3—the second-lowest among Magnificent Seven stocks—Microsoft presents a potential deep value opportunity as cloud computing grows at a projected 16% CAGR through 2033.

META.title: Meta Platforms Is Quietly Becoming One of the Market's Best AI Stocks to Buy. Here Are 3 Reasons Why.
META.claim: Meta's AI capabilities are significantly boosting its core advertising business, with Q1 revenue rising 33% year-over-year driven by AI-improved content ranking and ad optimization. The company can rapidly deploy AI features to 3.5 billion daily users, and its massive $125-145 billion capital expenditure for AI infrastructure is funded by strong profitability. Trading at 22x earnings, Meta appears attractively valued despite risks from delayed developer APIs and nascent monetization of AI agents.

META.title: Bargain Alert: This Tech Stock's Bold AI Strategy Could Unlock Explosive Upside
META.claim: Meta Platforms is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and developing its own MTIA chip program. While current AI spending appears substantial, the custom chip strategy could provide long-term efficiency gains and cost advantages across ranking, recommendations, advertising, and generative AI workloads, potentially transforming the margin outlook.

GOOGL.title: What Does Berkshire Hathaway See in This Housing Stock?
GOOGL.claim: Berkshire Hathaway's new CEO Greg Abel announced a $8.5 billion acquisition of homebuilder Taylor Morrison despite current weakness in the housing market. Abel is following Warren Buffett's contrarian investment philosophy by buying when others are fearful, betting on a cyclical recovery while the U.S. faces a shortage of 10 million homes.

GOOGL.title: 3 Elite Trillion-Dollar Giants Worth Loading Up On Right Now
GOOGL.claim: The article highlights three trillion-dollar companies—Walmart, Costco, and Berkshire Hathaway—as compelling investment opportunities. Walmart leverages AI and same-day delivery reaching 95% of U.S. households to drive growth. Costco maintains strong member loyalty with rising transaction sizes and membership fees. Berkshire Hathaway, under new CEO Greg Abel, is expanding into homebuilding and increasing its Alphabet stake, positioning itself as an underappreciated consumer story.

GOOGL.title: Goldman Sachs Says SpaceX Could Hit $474 Billion in Revenue by 2030. Here Are the 3 AI Stocks That Benefit Most.
GOOGL.claim: Goldman Sachs forecasts SpaceX will generate $474 billion in total revenue by 2030, with $322 billion from its AI division. Three companies stand to benefit significantly: Nvidia will supply advanced chips for SpaceX's data centers, Alphabet holds a 5-6% stake and rents computing capacity, and Tesla will benefit from a shared semiconductor manufacturing facility (Terafab) with SpaceX.

GOOGL.title: SpaceX Made Elon Musk a Trillionaire. Can SpaceX Stock Make You a Millionaire? (Hint: Yes, but There's a Catch)
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX's IPO on June 12, 2026 at $135/share valued the company at $2.1 trillion, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. While SpaceX has legitimate long-term potential through its diversified businesses in space exploration, Starlink broadband, and AI infrastructure, the article cautions that current valuations are inflated by IPO hype. The author recommends patience and waiting for lock-up expirations to subside before investing, as insider share releases could create downward pressure on the stock.

GOOGL.title: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. Engages Veteran Consumer Class Action and Complex Litigation Attorney C. Glen Ged as Strategic Legal Consultant
GOOGL.claim: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. has engaged C. Glen Ged, founder of Ged Lawyers LLP, as a strategic legal consultant to strengthen its litigation resources. The company is advancing four federal antitrust and consumer class action cases against major mobile carriers (AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile) and platform providers (Apple, Google, Samsung) in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia. Ged will provide strategic advice and help identify qualified consumer class action firms to pursue the matters on a contingency-fee basis.

GOOGL.title: Does the SpaceX IPO Mean You Should Ditch Rocket Lab?
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX's IPO has become the largest in history, causing investors to sell Rocket Lab shares, which dropped 32% from highs. While SpaceX has ambitious plans for AI data centers, Starlink, and Mars colonization, it trades at an extremely expensive 100+ times 2025 revenue. Rocket Lab, a smaller competitor focused on commercial rocket launches, is also expensive at 89x sales despite strong growth. The article recommends investors avoid both stocks due to their elevated valuations.

GOOGL.title: Meta to Follow Alphabet’s Footsteps? What an Equity Raise Could Mean
GOOGL.claim: Meta is reportedly considering an equity raise of tens of billions of dollars to fund AI infrastructure, potentially following Alphabet's $84.75 billion offering. However, Meta would face significantly higher shareholder dilution (~4.3%) compared to Alphabet (~1.9%) to raise a similar amount, which could negatively impact existing shareholders. While the capital could accelerate Meta's AI product development, the dilution effect and market reaction remain concerns.

GOOGL.title: Roku Is Being Acquired. Here's What Investors Need to Know.
GOOGL.claim: Fox Corporation has agreed to acquire Roku in a $22 billion cash-and-stock deal valued at $160 per share. The acquisition combines Fox's live news and sports portfolio with Roku's streaming platform, positioning the combined entity as a major player in connected TV. However, both stocks declined on the announcement, with Fox down 16% and Roku down 1%, as investors expressed concerns about Fox taking on $12 billion in new debt and the 34% premium paid for Roku.

GOOGL.title: Warren Buffett Successor Greg Abel Pours $10 Billion More Into His Largest Bet Yet
GOOGL.claim: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has invested $21 billion total in Alphabet shares, with $11 billion in Q1 and an additional $10 billion recently negotiated at a discount. This investment marks a significant shift from Warren Buffett's tech-averse strategy, positioning Alphabet as Berkshire's fourth-largest holding. The capital will fund AI infrastructure and employee compensation, supporting Google Cloud's accelerating growth and AI-driven improvements in the core advertising business.

GOOGL.title: Bull Market Pullback: Why the 4.5% Dip Held the 50-DMA
GOOGL.claim: The S&P 500 experienced a 4.5% pullback from its May 27 record high of 7,621 to 7,248 (the 50-day moving average), which the author characterizes as a healthy bull market correction rather than the start of a bear market. The bounce back to 7,431.46 on broad participation and positive money flows suggests the uptrend remains intact. However, the author warns of underlying risks including record margin debt ($1.30 trillion), declining retail demand, and inverted equity risk premium as 10-year Treasuries now yield more than S&P 500 earnings. The portfolio remains at 100% equity exposure based on positive money flow signals, but disciplined risk management is maintained ahead of historically weak summer months and midterm election volatility.

GOOGL.title: Warren Buffett's Successor, Greg Abel, Is Betting Big on a Virtual Monopoly That's About to Become Berkshire Hathaway's 4th-Largest Holding
GOOGL.claim: Greg Abel, Warren Buffett's successor as Berkshire Hathaway CEO, is significantly increasing the company's investment in Alphabet, making it Berkshire's 4th-largest holding. Berkshire committed $10 billion to Alphabet's $84.75 billion stock offering to fund AI infrastructure expansion. Abel is shifting Berkshire's strategy toward tech stocks, recognizing Alphabet's dominant position in search advertising and its emerging role as a leading AI applications deployer.

GOOGL.title: 3 Under-the-Radar Ways to Play Goldman's $1 Trillion AI Spending 2027 Forecast
GOOGL.claim: Goldman Sachs forecasts AI infrastructure spending could reach $920 billion to $1.4 trillion in 2027, up from $700 billion in 2026. The article identifies three stocks positioned to benefit: Alphabet (with cost advantages via TPUs), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC, with manufacturing monopoly), and ASML (sole provider of EUV lithography technology for chip production).

GOOGL.title: Consumer Tech News (Jun 8-12): SpaceX Debuts On Nasdaq, Big Tech Faces Political Scrutiny & More
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX debuted on Nasdaq with a 29% premium to its IPO price, making Elon Musk close to becoming a trillionaire. Big Tech faced political scrutiny with Senator Elizabeth Warren criticizing Meta's CEO and Nvidia's CEO declining to testify. Major tech companies reported earnings, announced layoffs, and made strategic pivots in AI investments and pricing strategies.

GOOGL.title: Berkshire Hathaway's Greg Abel Just Made a Big Bet on Who Will Win the AI Races -- And He's Even Tipping the Scale in Its Favor
GOOGL.claim: Berkshire Hathaway, under Greg Abel's leadership, has significantly increased its investment in Alphabet, tripling its stake in Q1 and committing an additional $10 billion to Alphabet's $80 billion equity raise. This makes Alphabet Berkshire's fourth-largest public equity holding. The move signals confidence that Alphabet will win the generative AI race, leveraging its cost advantages from owning data centers and designing its own chips (TPUs) compared to competitors like OpenAI and Anthropic.

GOOGL.title: Down 72%, Should You Buy the Dip in SoundHound AI?
GOOGL.claim: SoundHound AI stock has plunged 72% from all-time highs despite rapid business expansion with 52% YoY revenue growth. The company's voice AI technology is gaining traction with restaurants and automakers, and its new OASYS agentic AI platform has helped a Fortune 100 company save $10 million quarterly. However, the company remains unprofitable with a $26 million adjusted net loss and faces competition from tech giants like Google. The voice AI market is projected to reach $47 billion by 2034, offering significant long-term potential for patient investors willing to tolerate volatility.

GOOGL.title: Alphabet is Raising $84.75 Billion to Win the AI Wars. Should Investors Celebrate or Worry?
GOOGL.claim: Alphabet announced an $84.75 billion equity offering to fund its massive AI investments of $180-190 billion this year. While the company is seeing positive AI returns across its cloud business, Waymo, and Google Search, the equity raise will dilute existing shareholders by approximately 2%. The company could have used debt financing instead, but management deemed the dilution cheaper. Investors should view this favorably given AI's growth potential, but should be cautious if this becomes a recurring funding strategy.

GOOGL.title: Is Palantir Stock Ripe for a Rebound?
GOOGL.claim: Palantir Technologies has experienced strong revenue growth driven by its AI platform (AIP), with latest quarter revenue surging 85%. However, the stock has declined 26% this year due to high valuation concerns (89x forward earnings) and investor rotation away from growth stocks toward more stable sectors. While valuation has become more reasonable, the stock remains expensive compared to peers like Nvidia and Alphabet, though the company's strong fundamentals suggest potential for long-term gains.

GOOGL.title: Musk, Misunderstanding And SpaceX's Next Chapter: An Insider's View Of The Space Giant
GOOGL.claim: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell discussed the company's IPO pricing at $135 per share on IPO day, emphasizing long-term investor focus and retail participation (33% allocation). She highlighted major growth opportunities in Starship, Starlink, and Grok, positioned SpaceX as an infrastructure company building data centers on ground and in space, and addressed the $28.5 trillion total addressable market driven by AI, robotics, and autonomous vehicles.

NVDA.title: After Being Called the Next Trillion-Dollar AI Stock and Joining the S&P 500, Marvell's CFO Just Filed to Dump $65 Million of Stock. Here's Why.
NVDA.claim: Marvell Technology's CFO Willem Meintjes filed to sell approximately 211,000 shares worth $65 million following Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's endorsement calling Marvell the next trillion-dollar semiconductor stock and the company's S&P 500 inclusion announcement. However, the sale is attributed to Meintjes' planned departure after 10 years in the role, with an experienced successor already in place, suggesting the transaction reflects normal personal financial planning rather than underlying company concerns.

NVDA.title: Marvell Technology Skyrocketed on a Stunning Nvidia Endorsement: Could It Be the Next Trillion-Dollar AI Stock?
NVDA.claim: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang endorsed Marvell Technology as a potential trillion-dollar company, causing its stock to surge over 40% since early June. However, the article argues that despite strong growth prospects in AI networking and custom chips, Marvell's valuation at 76x forward earnings is too expensive compared to Nvidia at 24x forward earnings, making it a less attractive investment despite its industry tailwinds.

NVDA.title: Nvidia Just Raised $25 Billion in Debt. Here's What That Really Tells Investors
NVDA.claim: Nvidia raised $25 billion in bonds, its largest debt offering since 2021, despite holding $50 billion in cash and generating $48.6 billion in free cash flow in Q1. The move allows the company to lock in cheap long-term borrowing rates (4.25%-5.6%) while maintaining financial flexibility for shareholder returns and strategic investments. The strong investor demand for bonds maturing to 2056 signals confidence in sustained AI demand.

NVDA.title: Rumble Closes Acquisition of Northern Data
NVDA.claim: Rumble (NASDAQ: RUM) has completed its acquisition of Northern Data AG, gaining approximately 85.2% ownership. The deal provides Rumble with over 200 MW of unmonetized energy capacity, roughly 22,000 NVIDIA GPUs, and access to one of Europe's largest independent GPU estates. Northern Data raised its 2026 revenue outlook to 170-190 million euros from 130-150 million euros, with GPU utilization reaching 85%. Rumble also announced a $270 million multi-year contract with Together AI for GPU cloud capacity.

NVDA.title: Prediction: This Could Be Nvidia's Stock Price By the End of 2027
NVDA.claim: Nvidia could see its stock price reach $506 per share by end of 2027 based on CEO Jensen Huang's $1 trillion revenue visibility through 2027. The analysis projects 80% revenue growth in 2026 and 57% in 2027, driven by dominance in data center GPUs (85-92% market share) and rapid 12-18 month GPU generation cycles. At current valuations, Nvidia appears undervalued relative to its growth trajectory.

NVDA.title: Here’s What Jefferies Thinks About NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) - Insider Monkey
NVDA.claim: Here’s What Jefferies Thinks About NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA)    Insider Monkey

NVDA.title: 1 No-Brainer S&P 500 ETF to Buy Right Now for Less Than $500
NVDA.claim: The Vanguard S&P 500 Growth ETF (VOOG) is recommended as an attractive investment option for new and small-stake investors following a 6-for-1 stock split in April that reduced its price to around $81. The fund tracks 146 S&P 500 growth stocks with a low expense ratio of 0.07% and offers significant exposure to technology and AI-related companies, though investors should be aware of concentration risks in the tech sector.

NVDA.title: CoreWeave Stock Is Up Around 50% This Year, and Here's Why It Can Still Soar Higher
NVDA.claim: CoreWeave (CRWV) has gained approximately 50% in 2026 and will be added to the Nasdaq-100 index on June 22, which is expected to drive further gains through increased fund exposure and investor interest. The company rents compute space and provides access to Nvidia's chips, benefiting from surging AI demand. However, despite revenue growth from $1.9B to $5.1B year-over-year, CoreWeave remains unprofitable with a $1.6B net loss in the trailing 12 months, making it a higher-risk investment.

NVDA.title: Prediction: These 4 Words From Sam Altman Will Live on Infamy When It Comes to Artificial Intelligence
NVDA.claim: The article warns investors about unrealistic expectations in AI stocks. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman cautioned that people are 'begging to be disappointed' by AI capabilities, suggesting current valuations of AI-exposed stocks may not be justified if growth expectations fall short. The author recommends considering more value-oriented stocks with predictable business models rather than overweighting AI stocks.

NVDA.title: June 16 May be a Big Day for the Stock Market and Set the Stage for What Happens Next.
NVDA.claim: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh will oversee his first FOMC meeting on June 16, 2026, amid rising inflation concerns. Consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, the highest in three years, potentially complicating rate-cut expectations. While the Fed is expected to leave rates unchanged at this meeting, Warsh's policy direction—including potential balance sheet reduction and less forward guidance—could significantly impact the stock market, particularly given historically expensive valuations.

NVDA.title: Nvidia's China Woes Deepen As TikTok Parent ByteDance Eyes AI Chips From Iluvatar CoreX, Baidu: Report
NVDA.claim: ByteDance is in talks with Chinese chipmakers Iluvatar CoreX and Baidu to acquire AI chips, with Iluvatar CoreX projected to deliver 50,000 chips this year. This development reflects how Chinese tech firms are building domestic AI chip alternatives amid U.S. export restrictions on Nvidia, with Chinese GPU manufacturers now claiming nearly 41% of the domestic AI accelerator server market.

NVDA.title: SMH vs. SOXX vs. SOXQ: Which Semiconductor ETF Is the Best Buy Right Now?
NVDA.claim: Three major semiconductor ETFs—SMH, SOXX, and SOXQ—compete in the sector as tech giants plan $700 billion in capex spending for 2026. SMH offers the most concentrated mega-cap exposure with strong historical returns, SOXX provides more diversification but at higher cost, while SOXQ delivers similar diversification to SOXX at nearly half the expense ratio, making it the recommended choice for cost-conscious investors.

APLD.claim: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation contract with Applied Digital. Investors who purchased B&W securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 may be eligible for compensation.

AMZN.title: 3 Better Trillion-Dollar Stocks to Buy Instead of SpaceX
AMZN.claim: The article argues that Nvidia, Apple, and Amazon are better investment choices than SpaceX. While SpaceX is valued near $3 trillion based on speculative bets, these three companies have proven business models and stronger fundamentals. Nvidia dominates AI infrastructure with 85% revenue growth, Apple has a resilient ecosystem-based business, and Amazon is on track to reach $1 trillion in revenue by 2028 with established e-commerce and cloud computing leadership.

AMZN.title: Deep Learning Market to Reach $1,963.25 Billion by 2035 as Generative AI and Enterprise Automation Drive Massive AI Investments | Research by SNS Insider
AMZN.claim: The global deep learning market is projected to grow from $125.41 billion in 2025 to $1,963.25 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 31.69%. Growth is driven by generative AI adoption, enterprise automation, and increased AI infrastructure investments across healthcare, finance, automotive, and industrial sectors. The U.S. market is expected to expand from $37.14 billion to $596.02 billion, while Europe grows from $31.98 billion to $451.64 billion.

AMZN.title: Citizen Services AI Market Size to Hit USD 601.18 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
AMZN.claim: The global Citizen Services AI Market is expected to grow from $19.24 billion in 2025 to $601.18 billion by 2035 at a 41.13% CAGR. Growth is driven by digital government transformation mandates, demand for personalized citizen services, and AI-powered solutions for public service delivery. The U.S. market leads globally, projected to reach $256.42 billion by 2035, while Europe is expected to grow to $144.86 billion. Key applications include AI chatbots, fraud detection, and smart governance platforms.

AMZN.title: Amazon's Latest Warehouse Experiment Could Eliminate Millions Of Labor Hours: Report
AMZN.claim: Amazon is piloting a Full Facility Load Balancing (FFLB) system designed to optimize worker movement in its robot-filled warehouses. The system could save millions in labor costs and reduce approximately 7 million labor hours annually by automatically reassigning workers every three minutes based on package volumes. Amazon plans to roll out FFLB across its Amazon Robotic Sortable facilities this year and recently acquired Fauna Robotics, which developed a humanoid robot called Sprout.

AMZN.title: Goldman Sachs Warns Big Tech's $770 Billion AI Spending Frenzy Could Backfire— Here's Why
AMZN.claim: Goldman Sachs warns that major tech companies' massive $770 billion AI capital expenditure in 2026 could significantly harm profitability. The spending is transforming asset-light hyperscalers into capital-intensive enterprises, with depreciation costs expected to rise from 7% to 12% of revenues by 2027. This will compress profit margins and reduce Return on Equity by an average of 700 basis points for the seven largest tech stocks. However, strong Q1 earnings showing AI monetization success provides some optimism for long-term returns.

AMZN.title: SpaceX Joins Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Taiwan Semiconductor in the $2 Trillion Club. But Will It Last?
AMZN.claim: SpaceX reached a $2.11 trillion market cap at its IPO on June 12, becoming the seventh-most-valuable company globally. However, the article cautions that the stock's current valuation is driven by IPO hype and limited float rather than current fundamentals. With only $18.67 billion in 2025 revenue and a $4.94 billion net loss, SpaceX must grow into its valuation through Starlink expansion and AI monetization. The S&P 500 index committee declined to fast-track SpaceX's inclusion, suggesting concerns about overvaluation.

AMZN.title: FTC May Sue Amazon Over Advertising Misleading Claims (AMZN) - GuruFocus
AMZN.claim: FTC May Sue Amazon Over Advertising Misleading Claims (AMZN)    GuruFocus

AMZN.title: Meta Stock: Could Cloud Computing Be Its Next Move?
AMZN.claim: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg indicated that entering the cloud computing market is "definitely on the table," positioning it as a potential long-term option if the company builds excess AI capacity. While the cloud market is massive and growing (reaching $129 billion in Q1 2026), Meta would face significant challenges competing against established players like Microsoft, Amazon, and Google. The company would likely need substantial investments and could face years of losses before profitability, similar to Google Cloud's experience.

AMZN.title: Apple Will Raise Prices Due To Soaring Memory Costs: CEO Tim Cook Says 'This Is A 100-Year Flood'
AMZN.claim: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the company will raise prices across its product lineup due to skyrocketing memory and storage component costs. Cook described the situation as a "100-year flood," citing a dramatic reallocation of DRAM and NAND supply toward AI infrastructure by major tech companies. Memory chip prices have quadrupled as companies like Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon expand AI capex.

AMZN.title: The SpaceX IPO Could Trigger a Massive Rotation Across AI Stocks. Here's Why.
AMZN.claim: SpaceX's record $1.77 trillion IPO on June 12 is drawing investor capital away from AI stocks. The article argues SpaceX's 139x revenue valuation is unsustainably high for an unprofitable company, and predicts its eventual decline could trigger a rotation back to AI leaders like Nvidia and Broadcom. Recent declines in major AI stocks suggest investors are taking profits to fund SpaceX purchases. The outcome will also influence upcoming IPOs from OpenAI and Anthropic.

AMZN.claim: Amazon (AMZN) Faces FTC Lawsuit That Could Put Billions In Penalties At Stake    simplywall.st

AMZN.title: No Matter What Happens to the Market, These 3 Dividend Stocks Belong in Your Portfolio
AMZN.claim: The article recommends three Dividend King stocks (companies with 50+ consecutive years of dividend increases) as recession-resistant portfolio holdings: Altria Group for its pricing power despite declining smoking rates, Walmart for its dominant retail position and e-commerce growth, and Coca-Cola for its global beverage dominance and consistent earnings growth.

AMZN.title: As Capital Rotates From Crypto to AI, Zoomex Traders Already Have Access to Both
AMZN.claim: Zoomex, a crypto derivatives exchange, has launched Zoomex Stocks, offering tokenized versions of major U.S. equities and ETFs. The move reflects a significant market shift as institutional capital flows out of cryptocurrency into AI and semiconductor stocks, with Bitcoin ETFs experiencing $3.1 billion in year-to-date outflows while AI stocks surged 170%. The platform enables crypto traders to access equity exposure 24/7 using USDT with a flat 0.50% fee and near-instant settlement.

AMZN.title: Custom AI Chips Are Coming for Nvidia's Crown. Here Are 2 Companies Quietly Cashing In.
AMZN.claim: As major cloud companies design their own custom AI chips to reduce reliance on Nvidia, chip designers Broadcom and Marvell Technology are capturing significant revenue from this trend. Broadcom's AI revenue jumped 143% to $10.8 billion in its latest quarter with over $30 billion in booked AI orders, while Marvell's data center segment grew 27% with expectations for custom silicon to exceed $10 billion annually by fiscal 2029. Both companies trade at premium valuations but face customer concentration risks.

AMZN.title: SpaceX Is Now Bigger Than Amazon and Closing in on Microsoft. Here's How to Think About the Valuation.
AMZN.claim: SpaceX has become the fifth-largest company by market cap at $2.82 trillion following its IPO, surpassing Amazon and approaching Microsoft. The company's valuation is heavily driven by its AI division, which includes Grok, xAI, and planned orbital data centers. While Goldman Sachs projects 100-fold revenue growth by 2030, Morningstar assigns only a 7% probability to the company sustaining its current valuation, citing ambitious but unproven promises around orbital data centers and enterprise AI applications.

AMZN.title: 3 Ways to Invest in Anthropic Before It Goes Public
AMZN.claim: Anthropic, valued at $965 billion with $47 billion in annualized revenue, has confidentially filed for an IPO. Investors can gain indirect exposure through three public companies: Alphabet (committed to invest up to $40 billion and owns ~14%), Amazon (investing up to $25 billion total with planned $100 billion AWS spending), and Salesforce (owns ~$5 billion stake and integrates Claude into its AI platform).

AMZN.title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Runs A $5 Trillion Giant Like It's 30 Days From Collapse. Perplexity's CEO Says That's The Secret— 'You Need To Work Forever…'
AMZN.claim: Perplexity AI CEO Aravind Srinivas shared entrepreneurship lessons learned from Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and Tesla CEO Elon Musk. Srinivas highlighted Huang's approach of operating with constant urgency despite Nvidia's $5 trillion valuation, and Musk's mission-driven compensation structure at SpaceX focused on Mars colonization rather than wealth accumulation. Srinivas rejected the FIRE philosophy, advocating for continuous work and purpose-driven entrepreneurship.

AMZN.title: The SpaceX IPO Has Wall Street Debating Whether the AI Boom Is a Bubble. Both Sides Have a Point.
AMZN.claim: SpaceX's record $75 billion IPO has sparked debate about whether the AI boom is a bubble. Bears point to stretched valuations (S&P 500 cyclically adjusted P/E near 40, SpaceX trading at 90x revenue) and shrinking free cash flow at major tech companies despite massive AI spending. Bulls counter that massive cloud backlogs and unmet demand justify the investment, citing successful past technology cycles. The outcome depends on whether AI spending eventually produces sufficient profits.

AMZN.title: Apple Is Mostly Sitting Out the AI Spending Arms Race. With AI Stocks Selling Off, That Suddenly Looks Smart.
AMZN.claim: Apple's restrained approach to AI infrastructure spending ($12.7B capex vs. peers' $400B+) is being recast as prudent discipline as AI stocks sell off amid concerns about massive debt loads and cash burn. While Apple partners with Alphabet for its Siri AI overhaul, the company maintains strong cash generation and shareholder returns, though risks remain around user adoption and potential dependence on a rival's technology.

AMZN.title: Meet the 2 Newcomers Challenging the Cloud Computing Titans in Artificial Intelligence (AI)
AMZN.claim: CoreWeave and Nebius, specialized AI cloud computing companies, are experiencing explosive growth rates (112% and 684% respectively) compared to legacy cloud giants AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. While the established players are highly profitable, the newcomers are reinvesting all revenue into expansion. Wall Street expects CoreWeave and Nebius to maintain triple-digit growth through 2027, though profitability remains uncertain, presenting higher-risk but potentially higher-reward investment opportunities.

AMZN.title: 3 Core Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy With $1,000 Right Now and Hold for the Next Decade
AMZN.claim: The article recommends three AI stocks for long-term investment: Nvidia, Microsoft, and Amazon. Nvidia is positioned to benefit from massive data center capital expenditures ($1 trillion in 2027, growing to $3-4 trillion annually by 2030) and future GPU replacement cycles. Microsoft and Amazon are highlighted for their dominant cloud computing platforms (Azure and AWS) that will monetize AI through subscription-like revenue streams as clients rent computing capacity.

AMZN.title: My Top 5 Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks to Buy Right Now
AMZN.claim: The article recommends five AI stocks as strong investment opportunities, citing the expected AI infrastructure build-out through 2030. Nvidia leads as the top pick due to its dominant GPU market position. Sandisk benefits from memory chip shortages. Microsoft is positioned as a bargain after a 25% decline from its all-time high, with strong AI revenue growth. Meta is highlighted as a wildcard with potential upside from AI glasses and superintelligence projects. Amazon's AWS cloud computing division is expected to drive significant future growth.

AMZN.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide vs. GXO: Which Logistics Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
AMZN.claim: The article compares two logistics companies with different business models. C.H. Robinson Worldwide operates as an asset-light freight broker with strong profitability ($587M net income), robust free cash flow ($894.9M), and a healthy balance sheet (0.9x debt-to-equity). GXO Logistics focuses on tech-driven contract logistics with faster revenue growth (12.5% YoY) but struggles with profitability ($32M net income, 0.2% margin) and higher leverage (2.6x debt-to-equity). The author favors C.H. Robinson for its financial stability, operational flexibility, and exposure to improving LTL market pricing.

AMZN.title: SpaceX Just Made Its Market Debut. Here's What Investors Need to Know.
AMZN.claim: SpaceX completed the largest IPO on record, raising $75 billion with shares priced at $135, opening at $150, and surging over 20% on its first trading day. The company achieved a market valuation exceeding $2.1 trillion. While the IPO attracted significant retail investor participation and SpaceX operates in compelling growth markets (rocket launches, AI, satellite internet), the company faces challenges including substantial capital expenditures ($12 billion in AI alone last year), a $4.9 billion loss, and revenue of only $18 billion compared to trillion-dollar peers like Amazon.

AMZN.title: Tech Sell-Off: 2 Stocks to Buy Right Now
AMZN.claim: Following a tech sector pullback, Nvidia and Amazon are presented as attractive buying opportunities. Nvidia remains dominant in AI infrastructure with strong growth prospects in training, inference, and agentic AI. Amazon's AWS cloud business is accelerating with 28% revenue growth, while its e-commerce operations benefit from AI and robotics optimization.

AMZN.title: SpaceX Isn't Meta, It's Amazon, Says Investor Eyeing The IPO
AMZN.claim: Nancy Tengler, CEO of Laffer Tengler Investments, argues that SpaceX should be compared to Amazon rather than Meta as it approaches its IPO. She contends that SpaceX is a transformative technology company that should be evaluated on its long-term potential to reshape how people live and work, not on traditional valuation metrics. Tengler's firm has invested in SpaceX as part of a thematic portfolio focused on disruptive technologies, and she maintains a long-term investment horizon despite potential short-term volatility.

AMZN.title: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel Is Venturing Into an Area of the Stock Market That Warren Buffett Largely Shied Away From. Here's Why Investors Might Play Along.
AMZN.claim: New Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel is significantly increasing the conglomerate's investment in Alphabet, departing from Warren Buffett's traditional investment philosophy. Despite Alphabet's expected negative free cash flow due to AI infrastructure investments and valuations above historical averages, Abel's move reflects investor pressure to capitalize on the AI boom and deploy Berkshire's nearly $400 billion cash stockpile. The article suggests Alphabet is a relatively safer AI exposure given its diversified business segments.

HE.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie Says AI Restrictions Could Accelerate Global Shift To Open-Weight Models
HE.claim: Box CEO Aaron Levie warns that AI access restrictions and regulatory uncertainty are pushing governments and businesses toward open-weight AI models and sovereign AI development. He argues that regulatory focus on the model layer rather than applied layer creates geopolitical risks and incentivizes countries to develop independent AI systems, with open-weight models likely to benefit most as they are generally not U.S.-controlled.

SPGI.title: 1 Dividend King Stock Down 25% to Buy Right Now
SPGI.claim: S&P Global's stock has fallen 25% from its 52-week high due to AI disruption fears and disappointing 2026 earnings guidance. However, the company's dominant 50% market share in credit ratings, strong competitive moats, and 53-year dividend increase history make it an attractive buying opportunity at its lowest valuation since late 2022.

SPGI.title: Why First Advantage Stock Crushed the Market Today
SPGI.claim: First Advantage (FA) stock surged nearly 6% on Friday after being added to the S&P SmallCap 600 index, replacing Kennedy-Wilson Holdings which is being acquired. The inclusion makes the employment verification company an instant target for index funds and comes as the company has been performing well with double-digit growth in recent quarters.

SPGI.title: Is S&P Global the Best Wide-Moat Financial Stock to Buy Right Now?
SPGI.claim: S&P Global (SPGI) is presented as an attractive investment opportunity with multiple competitive moats across its credit ratings, indexing, and market intelligence businesses. Despite a 17% year-to-date decline driven by AI disruption concerns, the stock trades at a relatively cheap valuation of 21x forward earnings. The company demonstrated strong Q1 2026 performance with 10% revenue growth and 32% earnings growth, with full-year guidance of 6.3-8.3% revenue growth.

CME.title: The Fed's Latest Inflation Outlook Offers Wall Street Its First Relief in Months
CME.claim: The Federal Reserve's latest economic projections indicate interest rates will rise to 3.8% by year-end to combat persistent inflation expected at 3.6% in 2026. However, the Fed notes the economy remains resilient with solid growth, strong productivity, and stable employment. While inflation should cool significantly by 2027-2028, allowing rates to fall, the article cautions that stocks remain technically overbought and a market correction is due.

AMD.title: Is Marvell Technology Going to $1 Trillion?
AMD.claim: Marvell Technology has tripled year-to-date after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared it would be the next trillion-dollar company. The company posted strong 28% YoY revenue growth in Q1 FY27 with guidance implying 35% YoY growth in Q2, and CEO Matt Murphy expects accelerated growth throughout fiscal 2027 and 2028. New product innovations like the Teralynx T100 networking chip demonstrate competitive advantages, though other AI stocks like AMD and SanDisk may reach the $1 trillion milestone sooner.

AMD.title: 11 Words From Nvidia CFO Colette Kress That Should Have AMD and Intel Investors Worried
AMD.claim: Nvidia unveiled its Vera CPU platform designed for agentic AI workloads, opening a new $200 billion market opportunity. CFO Colette Kress indicated the company has visibility to nearly $20 billion in CPU revenue this year, with major hyperscalers already partnering for deployment. By offering a complete full-stack solution combining CPUs, GPUs, networking, and software, Nvidia is positioned to capture market share from traditional CPU incumbents and increase customer attachment rates.

AMD.title: Advanced Micro Devices vs. Arm Holdings: Which AI CPU Stock Is the Better Buy?
AMD.claim: AMD and Arm Holdings are both positioned to benefit from growing AI CPU demand, particularly in inference and agentic AI workloads. AMD's EPYC CPUs are already seeing strong adoption with 57% data center revenue growth and expectations for 70%+ year-over-year server CPU growth. Arm is expanding beyond licensing into finished chips with its AGI CPU, backed by over $2 billion in customer demand. However, both stocks trade at premium valuations (AMD at 75x and Arm at 179x forward earnings). AMD appears more attractive for near-term revenue visibility, while Arm offers longer-term platform potential with higher execution risk.

AMD.title: TinyML AI Chipset Shipments to Top 4.1 Billion by 2031 as Embedded AI Scales Across Industrial IoT
AMD.claim: ABI Research forecasts TinyML AI chipset shipments will reach 4.1 billion units by 2031 with revenue exceeding $7.8 billion, growing at 37% CAGR. NPUs show fastest growth at 90% CAGR while MCUs lead the segment. Asia-Pacific will exceed 721 million shipments. Premium smartphones remain resilient while mid-to-low-priced models face pressure from higher DRAM costs. Heterogeneous SoC architectures gain traction as vendors optimize AI workloads across CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs.

AMD.title: AMD Is Up 149% Year to Date. Is the AI Chip Rally Getting Dangerously Crowded, or Is There Still Room to Run?
AMD.claim: AMD's stock has surged 149% year-to-date with Q1 2026 revenue reaching $10.3 billion (up 38% YoY) and data center revenue at $5.8 billion (up 57% YoY). The company guided for Q2 2026 revenue of $11.2 billion (46% YoY growth) and is preparing to launch MI450 AI chips with major customer commitments from OpenAI and Meta. Despite the stock's significant gains, the company's strong earnings growth and expanding AI market share suggest further upside potential for long-term investors.

AMD.title: Is Intel the Comeback Stock of the Decade?
AMD.claim: Intel has experienced a remarkable sevenfold stock price increase over 14 months, rising from $18.84 to around $130 per share, driven by CEO Lip-Bu Tan's execution of the foundry strategy, the 18A process node success, CHIPS Act funding, and growing data center CPU demand. While other stocks like Carvana have achieved higher percentage returns, Intel's comeback is arguably more significant from a macro perspective as it strengthens U.S. semiconductor competitiveness and reduces dependence on Taiwan.

AMD.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Leads AI Hardware Market with Revenue Growth - GuruFocus

AMD.title: 3 AI Stocks Poised for Long-Term Gains Despite Strong Year-to-Date Performance
AMD.claim: Three AI stocks—Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), Alphabet, and Nvidia—are highlighted as solid long-term winners despite recent volatility. TSMC dominates semiconductor manufacturing for AI processors, Alphabet's Gemini AI is expanding across its services and generating revenue, and Nvidia maintains 86% market share in AI data center processors with strong growth prospects in robotics and autonomy.

AMD.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Appeared Destined for the $1 Trillion Club. Here's Why It Lost Momentum.
AMD.claim: Oracle's AI data center business showed strong growth with $638 billion in remaining performance obligations, but the stock has fallen 44% from its all-time high due to concerns about whether major customers like OpenAI can fulfill their commitments. With $122 billion in long-term debt and only 12% of RPO expected to convert to revenue in the next 12 months, investors are advised to take a wait-and-see approach.

AMD.title: Is Nvidia Stock a Buy?
AMD.claim: Nvidia continues to deliver exceptional business results with 85% revenue growth and accelerating data center demand driven by agentic AI. However, the stock has stalled near all-time highs despite strong fundamentals. While the valuation appears reasonable at 31x P/E for a company growing this fast, investors should be cautious due to China revenue risks, potential competition from major cloud provider customers building their own chips, and AMD's advancing AI accelerators.

TSLA.title: This Shocking Company Just Passed Ford -- and Its Sales Could More Than Double
TSLA.claim: BYD, a Chinese EV maker, has surpassed Ford in global automotive sales and become the world's top-selling EV maker. With 4.6 million vehicles sold and 7.7% growth, BYD's CEO claims the company can become the No. 1 global automaker within five years, driven by new Blade Battery 2.0 and Flash Charging technologies that have generated over 100,000 orders for some models.

TSLA.title: SpaceX Stock Faces Post-IPO Valuation Risks as AST SpaceMobile, Rocket Lab Rebound
TSLA.claim: SpaceX's IPO has driven its stock from $135 to over $225, creating a $2.5 trillion valuation driven by retail options trading and float scarcity rather than fundamentals. The company trades at 20x projected 2027 sales while posting $4.94 billion annual losses. A $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Cursor and Tesla merger rumors inflate valuations further. Meanwhile, AST SpaceMobile and Rocket Lab offer better risk-reward profiles after being temporarily overshadowed by the SpaceX IPO frenzy.

TSLA.title: Elon Musk Just Delivered Fantastic News for ASML Stock Investors
TSLA.claim: Elon Musk addressed ASML employees about the Terafab initiative, a joint venture between Tesla and SpaceX to build a massive semiconductor manufacturing complex with a proposed $55-119 billion investment. This signals strong future demand for ASML's essential EUV lithography machines used in AI chip production, validating the company's strategic importance to both companies' supply chains.

TSLA.title: When Will Starlink Officially Announce An IPO? Here's What Prediction Market Is Saying
TSLA.claim: Following SpaceX's successful IPO that made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire, attention turns to whether Starlink will go public. Prediction market data from Kalshi shows only 7% probability of a Starlink IPO before April 1, 2027, and 10% before June 30, 2027. Meanwhile, calls for a Tesla-SpaceX merger have grown among investors, and Musk projects SpaceX could reach $1 trillion in annual revenue by 2030.

CBRS.title: Cerebras vs. SpaceX: Which Is the Better AI IPO Stock to Buy and Hold for the Next 10 Years?
CBRS.claim: Cerebras and SpaceX are two major AI-linked IPOs with different business models. Cerebras specializes in wafer-scale AI processors for fast inference with strong OpenAI backing ($10B+ deal), while SpaceX leverages Starlink's recurring revenue base and expanding AI infrastructure business. SpaceX is recommended as the stronger 10-year pick due to multiple growth engines and lower dependency on single customers, though Cerebras offers higher upside potential if it executes on its ambitious roadmap.

NDAQ.title: SpaceX Can Be Added to the Russell 1000 and Russell 3000 After Today -- Don't Take the Bait
NDAQ.claim: SpaceX completed a record $75 billion IPO and will be added to the Russell 1000 and Russell 3000 indexes after just 5 trading days due to new expedited inclusion rules. However, the article warns retail investors to avoid the stock, citing concerns about artificially inflated prices from forced index fund purchases, an unorthodox insider lockup schedule that allows insiders to dump shares starting in August, and fundamental valuation concerns.

NDAQ.title: SpaceX Has Successfully Completed Its IPO. Here Are All of the Key Dates Investors Should Be Aware of Over the Next 180 Days.
NDAQ.claim: SpaceX completed its IPO, raising $86 billion and achieving a $2.5 trillion market cap, making it the largest IPO in history. Over the next 180 days, the stock will be added to major indexes (driving demand) while insider lock-up provisions expire in stages (increasing supply). The author suggests investors wait until after 180 days to get a true valuation as these artificial market forces balance out.
